project.name: clawmark
project.description: A local Rust CLI that A/B tests two CLAUDE.md files against a SWE-bench Lite smoke set.

nodes.name: CI
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI pipeline that triggers on every push and pull request. Runs on ubuntu-latest, installs the stable Rust toolchain with rustfmt and clippy components, then executes `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` in sequence to enforce formatting, lint, and test coverage for the clawmark CLI.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Git ignore rules excluding build artifacts (/target), output directories (/out, /out-e2e*), local variant files (/variants), scripts, virtual environments, planning docs (.planning/), IDE config (.cursor/), and the local-only AGENTS.local.md.

nodes.name: AGENTS.local.md
nodes.summary: Local (gitignored) AI agent context file that instructs agents to read a personal Obsidian note for the clawmark feature roadmap and implementation priority order before working on new features.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Primary AI agent entry point documenting v1 project priorities, architecture constraints, and workflow rules for clawmark. Covers dependency minimization, no-scope-creep policy, fixed smoke task set, CLI-only design, serial execution, and quick-orientation reference for Claude invocation, Docker, and subprocess safety.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Minimal CLAUDE.md that redirects all AI agent context to AGENTS.md as the canonical entry point.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Contributor guidelines: PRs welcome, open an issue for major changes first, build with `cargo build` and test with `cargo test`, update tests as appropriate.

nodes.name: Cargo.lock
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Cargo lockfile pinning exact dependency versions for reproducible builds. Derived from Cargo.toml and should not be manually edited.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Rust package manifest for clawmark v0.1.0 (edition 2021, MSRV 1.79). Declares dependencies: clap (derive+env), serde (derive), serde_json, tempfile, sha2, hex, and tokio (rt-multi-thread+sync). Enforces unsafe_code=forbid, all+pedantic clippy warnings, and release profile with strip+LTO.

nodes.name: LICENSE
nodes.summary: MIT License for clawmark, copyright 2026 Emilio Lugo. Grants broad permission to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and sell with no warranty.

nodes.name: PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Maintainer-local (gitignored) v1 implementation plan covering: v1 contract (doctor/run/report subcommands), prerequisites, bundled 5-task SWE-bench Lite smoke set, run limits, CLI validation rules, security model, architecture overview, output file layout, harness invocation, crate layout, key Rust types, Claude invocation spec, error/retry policy, cleanup behavior, report metrics, and parallel workstream delegation across phased milestones.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary public-facing project overview for clawmark. Covers project purpose (A/B testing two CLAUDE.md files on SWE-bench Lite), v1 feature scope, prerequisites table, quickstart, runtime and budget warnings, how runs work, CLI reference (doctor/run/report), input rules, output file layout, failure behavior, security model, troubleshooting, telemetry disclaimer, and contributing/license links.

nodes.name: PROJECT.md
nodes.summary: GSD planning framework project definition document for clawmark. Captures the high-level project identity, purpose, and scope for the A/B CLAUDE.md benchmarking CLI.

nodes.name: REQUIREMENTS.md
nodes.summary: GSD planning framework requirements document for clawmark. Defines functional and non-functional requirements for the local Rust CLI that A/B tests CLAUDE.md files against SWE-bench Lite.

nodes.name: ROADMAP.md
nodes.summary: GSD planning framework roadmap document for clawmark. Outlines phased milestones and delivery timeline for the smoke runner, report command, and full SWE-bench Lite integration.

nodes.name: STATE.md
nodes.summary: GSD planning framework live state tracker for clawmark. Records current phase, active tasks, blockers, and progress checkpoints used by the GSD workflow system.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: GSD planning framework configuration file for clawmark. Stores planning system settings such as project name, active milestone, and workflow preferences.

nodes.name: 01-01-PLAN.md
nodes.summary: GSD execution plan for Phase 01 (parallel execution): adds --parallel N to clawmark run using tokio Semaphore-based task fan-out, Mutex-serialized JSONL writes, and an async run_ab entry point. Defines two implementation tasks, a threat model (T-01-01 DoS, T-01-02 write tampering, T-01-SC supply chain), must-have truths covering auth-failure semantics and ordering, and seven review findings (F-01–F-07) with their dispositions.

nodes.name: 01-01-SUMMARY.md
nodes.summary: Completion summary for GSD plan 01-01: both tasks finished (tokio dependency + --parallel CLI flag; semaphore-based concurrent task execution in runner). Documents key changes to Cargo.toml, src/cli.rs, src/runner.rs, and src/main.rs. Confirms 37 tests pass, clean release build, and --parallel visible in help output.

nodes.name: 01-REVIEWS.md
nodes.summary: Cross-AI code review (Cursor) of the Phase 01 parallel-execution plan. Rates overall risk MEDIUM. Highlights two HIGH findings (auth-failure abort semantics conflict with 'exactly one record per task'; fan-out spawns all tasks upfront so queued tasks cannot be cheaply cancelled), two MEDIUM findings (nondeterministic output ordering; parallel==1 identity weaker than claimed), and two LOW findings (tokio time feature inconsistency; weak DoS argument). Provides concrete suggestions for each.

nodes.name: 01-VERIFICATION.md
nodes.summary: Verification report for Phase 01 parallel execution (verified 2026-06-19, status: passed). 3/3 roadmap success criteria verified; 7/7 must-have truths checked (5 fully verified, 2 structurally present but runtime-behavior unverified: concurrent write serialization and queued-task behavior after auth failure). All required artifacts confirmed in source. CONC-01 fully satisfied.

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Entry point for the clawmark CLI. Parses arguments via clap, builds a tokio multi-thread runtime for the Run subcommand, and dispatches to doctor::run_doctor, runner::run_ab, or report functions. Exits with an integer code reflecting success or failure.

nodes.name: cli.rs
nodes.summary: Defines all CLI argument structs (Cli, Commands, RunArgs, ReportArgs, ValidatedRunArgs) using clap derive macros. Contains validation logic that canonicalizes variant paths, enforces cwd containment, rejects duplicate paths, validates model string, timeout range, and output directory constraints.

nodes.name: doctor.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the 'doctor' subcommand: checks for docker, claude CLI, claude authentication, python 3.11+, swebench package, harness CLI, git, Docker registry TCP reachability, and SWE-bench Docker image availability. Renders a tabular PASS/FAIL/WARN status report.

nodes.name: runner.rs
nodes.summary: Core A/B runner. Orchestrates sandbox creation, CLAUDE.md injection, claude CLI invocation with timeout, SWE-bench harness execution, and result collection. Supports parallel task execution via tokio semaphore. Defines VariantSlot, RunKey, RunRecord, and the ClaudeOutcome private enum.

nodes.name: report.rs
nodes.summary: Reads harness output files (harness/a.json, harness/b.json) and computes A vs B win/loss/tie statistics over SMOKE_INSTANCE_IDS. Provides terminal table rendering, optional per-task patch display, and atomic JSON report writing. Defines Report and TaskOutcome types.

nodes.name: results.rs
nodes.summary: Shared data types and I/O utilities. Defines SwebenchPrediction and HarnessResult. Provides atomic JSON writes via NamedTempFile, JSONL append/read for RunRecords, predictions JSONL writer, and harness result loader. Exports path constants and the REPORT_REQUIRED_FILES sentinel.

nodes.name: sandbox.rs
nodes.summary: Creates isolated temp workspaces by git-cloning the task repo at the base commit, injects the CLAUDE.md variant file as a plain file write, and collects the model patch via 'git diff HEAD'. All git operations use argv arrays, never shell strings. Workspace is RAII via TempDir.

nodes.name: swebench.rs
nodes.summary: Defines SWE-bench data types (TaskInstance, Prediction) and the fixed 5-task smoke set constant SMOKE_INSTANCE_IDS. Provides loaders for the bundled compile-time embedded JSONL dataset and input validation (repo slug format, 40-hex base commit).

nodes.name: Cli
nodes.summary: Top-level clap Parser struct. Holds the Commands enum and drives subcommand dispatch for doctor, run, and report.

nodes.name: Commands
nodes.summary: Subcommand enum: Doctor (no args), Run(RunArgs), Report(ReportArgs). Drives the match in main.

nodes.name: RunArgs
nodes.summary: Raw CLI arguments for the run subcommand: --a, --b (variant paths), --model, --timeout-secs, --out, --parallel. Implements validate() producing ValidatedRunArgs and validate_with_cwd() for testability.

nodes.name: ReportArgs
nodes.summary: Raw CLI arguments for the report subcommand: --out and --show-patches. Implements validate() checking that the output directory and required harness files exist.

nodes.name: ValidatedRunArgs
nodes.summary: Canonicalized and validated run args: a_canonical, b_canonical (canonical paths within cwd), model string, timeout_secs, out dir, and parallel concurrency count.

nodes.name: CheckStatus
nodes.summary: Enum with variants Pass, Fail, Warn. Classifies the outcome of each prerequisite check. Only Fail increments the exit code.

nodes.name: CheckResult
nodes.summary: Struct holding a check's static name, CheckStatus, and a human-readable message string for display in the status table.

nodes.name: VariantSlot
nodes.summary: Enum with variants A and B. Provides label() ("a"/"b"), model_name_or_path() ("clawmark/a"/"clawmark/b"), and run_id() ("clawmark-a"/"clawmark-b") helpers.

nodes.name: RunKey
nodes.summary: Composite identity key for a single run record: the VariantSlot, a SHA-256 hex hash of the variant file contents, and the task instance_id string.

nodes.name: RunRecord
nodes.summary: Complete serializable record for one Claude invocation: schema_version, RunKey, Prediction, elapsed_secs (f64), and optional error message. Written as one JSONL line to run_records.jsonl.

nodes.name: SwebenchPrediction
nodes.summary: Harness-format prediction struct: instance_id, model_patch, model_name_or_path. Convertible from swebench::Prediction via a From impl.

nodes.name: HarnessResult
nodes.summary: Deserialized harness output containing resolved_ids: Vec<String> — the list of instance IDs the harness judged as passing.

nodes.name: TaskInstance
nodes.summary: SWE-bench task definition: instance_id, repo slug (owner/name), 40-hex base_commit, problem_statement, optional hints_text, and version string.

nodes.name: Prediction
nodes.summary: Model prediction output stored in RunRecord: instance_id, model_patch (git unified diff), and model_name_or_path identifier.

nodes.name: Report
nodes.summary: Full A/B report: schema_version, total_tasks, a_resolved/b_resolved counts, a_wins, b_wins, ties_both_resolved, ties_both_failed, and a Vec<TaskOutcome> for per-task details.

nodes.name: TaskOutcome
nodes.summary: Per-task A/B outcome: instance_id string, a_resolved bool, b_resolved bool.

nodes.name: Workspace
nodes.summary: RAII handle for a cloned git repository in a TempDir. The TempDir field ensures the directory is deleted when the Workspace drops. The path field points to the repo subdirectory inside the temp dir.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses Cli via clap, matches on Commands. For Doctor: calls run_doctor. For Run: validates args, builds a tokio multi-thread runtime, and block_on(runner::run_ab). For Report: calls compute_report, render_terminal_table, optionally render_patches, then write_report_json.

nodes.name: run_ab
nodes.summary: Async orchestrator for the full A/B run. Loads the bundled smoke set, hashes both variant files, creates output directories, serially calls run_variant for A then B, invokes harness for both, and computes and prints the final report.

nodes.name: run_variant
nodes.summary: Async function running one variant against all smoke tasks. Uses a tokio semaphore for concurrency control and spawn_blocking for run_single. Collects predictions and writes run_records.jsonl entries under a Mutex.

nodes.name: run_single
nodes.summary: Synchronous function executed via spawn_blocking. Creates a sandbox, injects CLAUDE.md, invokes Claude CLI, collects the git diff patch, and returns a RunRecord with errors stored in the record rather than propagated (unless it is an auth failure).

nodes.name: invoke_claude
nodes.summary: Spawns the claude CLI with argv: -p, --output-format json, --dangerously-skip-permissions, --model, --add-dir <workspace>, -- <problem_statement>. Polls with try_wait enforcing timeout. Returns ClaudeOutcome::Auth on auth-failure strings to abort the whole run.

nodes.name: invoke_harness
nodes.summary: Runs python3 -m swebench.harness.run_evaluation with dataset, split, predictions_path, instance_ids, max_workers=1, run_id, and timeout. After success, calls finalize_harness_summary to copy the raw output to the stable harness/{a,b}.json path.

nodes.name: run_doctor
nodes.summary: Calls collect_check_results to run all nine prerequisite checks, renders the status table, and returns exit code 1 if any check has CheckStatus::Fail.

nodes.name: compute_report
nodes.summary: Loads harness/a.json and harness/b.json via load_harness_results, then delegates to aggregate_report to produce a Report.

nodes.name: aggregate_report
nodes.summary: Pure function. Iterates over SMOKE_INSTANCE_IDS and compares resolved_ids sets from two HarnessResult values. Accumulates a_wins, b_wins, ties_both_resolved, ties_both_failed counts and builds per-task TaskOutcome entries.

nodes.name: create
nodes.summary: Validates repo slug and base commit format, creates a TempDir, git-clones the GitHub repo URL, checks out the exact base commit, and returns a Workspace. All git calls use argv arrays.

nodes.name: inject_claude_md
nodes.summary: Writes the variant byte slice as CLAUDE.md at the workspace repo root using std::fs::write. This is how the A/B variant is introduced into each task's isolated environment.

nodes.name: collect_patch
nodes.summary: Runs 'git diff HEAD' in the workspace and returns the unified diff string. An empty string is a valid result meaning the model made no changes (unresolved task).

nodes.name: load_bundled_smoke_set
nodes.summary: Parses the compile-time embedded JSONL dataset (via include_str! of data/swebench_lite_v1_subset.jsonl) and returns the five TaskInstances for the fixed smoke set.

nodes.name: validate_repo_slug
nodes.summary: Validates a GitHub owner/name repo slug: requires exactly one '/' separator and only alphanumeric, underscore, dot, or dash characters in each part. Prevents shell injection in constructed git URLs.

nodes.name: write_atomic_json
nodes.summary: Serializes a value to pretty JSON and atomically writes it to the target path via a NamedTempFile in the same directory, then persists it. Prevents partial writes on crash.

nodes.name: load_harness_results
nodes.summary: Opens a harness JSON file and extracts the resolved_ids array into a HarnessResult, returning explicit errors for missing or wrong-type fields.

nodes.name: append_run_record
nodes.summary: Serializes a RunRecord to a JSON string and appends it as a single JSONL line to the run_records.jsonl file, creating the file and parent directories if needed.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md (Claude Code config)
nodes.summary: GSD-managed Claude Code agent instructions for the clawmark project. Defines the project description (A/B benchmarking CLAUDE.md variants against SWE-bench Lite), tech stack, conventions, architecture notes, workflow enforcement rules, and developer profile placeholder. Auto-generated sections are managed by GSD tooling and must not be edited manually.

nodes.name: settings.local.json
nodes.summary: Local Claude Code permissions configuration that explicitly allows WebSearch, a scoped Bash command (rtk read *), and the gsd-execute-phase skill. Restricts the tool surface available to Claude Code agents running in this project.

nodes.name: swebench_lite_v1_subset.jsonl
nodes.summary: Bundled dataset of exactly 5 SWE-bench Lite task instances comprising the fixed astropy smoke set: astropy__astropy-12907, 14182, 14365, 14995, and 6938. Each line is a JSON object with instance_id, repo, base_commit, and problem_statement. Loaded by the runner at runtime as the sole benchmark input for A/B runs.

nodes.name: e2e_smoke.sh
nodes.summary: Bash end-to-end smoke test script that replicates clawmark's live execution path for a single task without the full SWE-bench Docker harness. Clones the target repo, checks out the base commit, injects a CLAUDE.md variant, invokes the claude CLI with the same argv as clawmark, captures git diff HEAD, and asserts a non-empty patch was produced. Accepts model, timeout, and variant path as positional arguments.

nodes.name: variants/a.md (Variant A)
nodes.summary: Variant A CLAUDE.md instructions. Directs Claude to apply the smallest possible code change to fix the described issue, explicitly prohibiting test runs, refactoring, feature additions, and reformatting. Designed to test whether a minimal-change strategy yields better SWE-bench resolution rates.

nodes.name: variants/b.md (Variant B)
nodes.summary: Variant B CLAUDE.md instructions. Directs Claude to solve the issue directly with small patches and verify behavior with tests. Represents a test-driven strategy intended to be compared against Variant A on the same SWE-bench Lite smoke tasks.

edges.description: CLAUDE.md redirects all agent context to AGENTS.md as the canonical entry point
AGENTS.md links to CLAUDE.md for project-level coding guidelines
AGENTS.md cites README.md and per-module doc comments as the source of truth for contributors
AGENTS.md references PLAN.md as the full phased implementation plan and dependency rationale table

README.md links to LICENSE for the MIT license text
Cargo.lock is the auto-generated lockfile derived from and pinning versions declared in Cargo.toml
.gitignore excludes PLAN.md (via .planning/ pattern), keeping the implementation plan maintainer-local
.gitignore explicitly excludes AGENTS.local.md to keep local agent context private
CONTRIBUTING.md instructs contributors to build and test using cargo, which depends on Cargo.toml

Project definition drives roadmap planning

Roadmap milestones are tracked in current state

Smoke script defaults VARIANT to variants/a.md; injects the selected variant as CLAUDE.md into the cloned repo before invoking claude.
Smoke script accepts any variant path as its third positional argument, including variants/b.md.
Both files are competing CLAUDE.md variant prompts being A/B tested against the same SWE-bench Lite smoke tasks.
Both are Claude Code local configuration files co-located under .claude/ that together define the agent context and allowed tool permissions for this project.
The project CLAUDE.md describes the purpose of clawmark as an A/B benchmark of CLAUDE.md variants; variants/a.md is one of the two files being compared.
The project CLAUDE.md describes the purpose of clawmark as an A/B benchmark of CLAUDE.md variants; variants/b.md is the other file being compared.
The CI pipeline runs cargo fmt, cargo clippy, and cargo test — all driven by the Cargo.toml manifest.
swebench.rs embeds the JSONL dataset at compile time via include_str!, making it the sole runtime source of the fixed 5-task smoke set.

layers.name: Core Logic Layer
layers.description: Subcommand implementations for doctor, run, and report

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Bundled benchmark datasets and A/B variant files

layers.name: Build & Config
layers.description: Cargo manifests, lockfile, git and tool configuration

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project-level documentation and agent context files

layers.name: Planning
layers.description: GSD workflow planning documents, state, and phase artifacts

tour.title: What clawmark Does
tour.description: Start with the README and the agent-context files to understand the problem clawmark solves. The project answers one focused question: which of two CLAUDE.md files makes Claude perform better on real software-engineering bugs? v1 compares exactly two local variant files (Variant A and Variant B) against a fixed set of five SWE-bench Lite tasks from the astropy repository, runs Claude locally, evaluates the generated patches with the official SWE-bench harness in Docker, and prints a simple A/B summary. AGENTS.md captures all guiding constraints for the project—minimal dependencies, no scope creep, ship the smoke runner first—and is the canonical source of truth for contributors and AI agents alike.

tour.title: Entry Point: main.rs
tour.description: main.rs is the executable entry point. It parses the top-level CLI struct with Clap, then dispatches to one of three subcommand handlers: doctor::run_doctor(), runner::run_ab(), or report::compute_report(). The run subcommand is the only one that builds a Tokio multi-thread runtime—needed for async parallel Claude invocations—while doctor and report are fully synchronous. All three return an integer exit code; main.rs calls std::process::exit with it so the shell sees success or failure.

tour.title: CLI Argument Parsing & Validation: cli.rs
tour.description: cli.rs defines the three Clap structs—Cli, RunArgs, and ReportArgs—and the Commands enum that main.rs matches on. The most important concept here is the two-phase design: raw args (RunArgs) are parsed by Clap and then validated by validate_with_cwd() to produce a ValidatedRunArgs that guarantees invariants the rest of the code depends on. Validation checks that --a and --b resolve to different files inside the current working directory, that --timeout-secs is in range, that the output directory does not already exist (runs are never resumed or overwritten), and that --parallel is at least 1. For report, validate() confirms that harness/a.json and harness/b.json already exist before any I/O is attempted.

tour.title: Prerequisite Checker: doctor.rs
tour.description: doctor.rs implements the doctor subcommand, which probes the local environment before a user attempts a full run. It checks that docker, the claude CLI, python3 (with the swebench package importable), and git are all present and functional. Each check is a simple subprocess invocation—no shell strings, always argv arrays. Failures are printed clearly with actionable messages. Running clawmark doctor before clawmark run is the recommended first step documented in the README, because the SWE-bench harness requires Docker and the claude CLI requires local authentication.

tour.title: The Benchmark Dataset: swebench.rs & the Bundled JSONL
tour.description: swebench.rs defines the two core data types—TaskInstance (one SWE-bench problem: repo, base commit, and problem statement) and Prediction (a generated patch)—and owns the five fixed smoke instance IDs in the SMOKE_INSTANCE_IDS constant. The entire bundled dataset is embedded at compile time via include_str!, so no external file access is required at runtime. load_bundled_smoke_set() parses the embedded JSONL string line by line into Vec<TaskInstance>. The data file itself, data/swebench_lite_v1_subset.jsonl, contains exactly the five astropy tasks that v1 always runs in fixed order. The module also provides validate_repo_slug and validate_base_commit to guard against path-traversal or injection before any git URL is constructed.

tour.title: Workspace Sandbox: sandbox.rs
tour.description: sandbox.rs is responsible for the per-task, per-variant isolated workspace. create() clones the task's GitHub repository at the exact base commit into a tempfile::TempDir, ensuring the workspace is automatically cleaned up when the Workspace struct is dropped. inject_claude_md() then writes the chosen variant's contents as CLAUDE.md at the repo root—this is the mechanism by which the A/B test works, since Claude auto-discovers CLAUDE.md for instructions. After Claude exits, collect_patch() runs git diff HEAD to capture any changes Claude made; an empty diff is a valid result (the task was not resolved). All git invocations use argv arrays with no shell interpolation.

tour.title: Shared Types & Serialization: results.rs
tour.description: results.rs is the I/O and type backbone shared between runner.rs and report.rs. It defines HarnessResult (the structure the SWE-bench harness writes, keyed on resolved_ids), SwebenchPrediction (the format the harness expects as input), and named path constants for every output file (run_records.jsonl, predictions/a.jsonl, harness/a.json, report.json, etc.). Key functions: variant_hash() computes a SHA-256 fingerprint of the variant file contents so each RunRecord is traceable to an exact variant; append_run_record() appends JSONL records atomically; write_predictions_jsonl() uses a NamedTempFile then persists to avoid partial writes; load_harness_results() reads and validates the narrow resolved_ids-only schema from harness output.

tour.title: Core A/B Runner: runner.rs
tour.description: runner.rs orchestrates the entire benchmark run. run_ab() is the async top-level: it loads the five tasks, reads and hashes both variant files, creates the output directory tree, then calls run_variant() for A then B serially. run_variant() fans out over tasks using a Tokio semaphore to bound concurrency (--parallel controls the semaphore size), spawning each task as a spawn_blocking call so the blocking git/claude subprocesses don't block the async runtime. run_single() is the per-task unit: sandbox.create → sandbox.inject_claude_md → invoke_claude → sandbox.collect_patch → produce RunRecord. invoke_claude() builds the exact claude argv (-p --output-format json --dangerously-skip-permissions --model <model> --add-dir <workspace> -- <problem_statement>), polls the child process with a 100ms spin loop, and distinguishes auth failures (which abort the whole run) from per-task failures (which are stored on the record). After both variants finish, invoke_harness() runs the SWE-bench Python harness in Docker for each, then the report is computed and printed inline.

tour.title: A/B Report Generation: report.rs
tour.description: report.rs reads harness/a.json and harness/b.json, treats their resolved_ids arrays as the sole source of truth, and computes per-task outcomes. For each of the five smoke tasks it checks whether A resolved it, B resolved it, both, or neither, yielding four aggregate counters: a_wins, b_wins, ties_both_resolved, ties_both_failed. render_terminal_table() prints the human-readable summary. write_report_json() persists the Report struct to report.json via write_atomic_json. The report subcommand lets you re-render this table from a completed output directory without re-running anything; the run subcommand also calls these functions inline after the harness finishes.

tour.title: Variant Files: What You're Actually Comparing
tour.description: The whole point of clawmark is to compare two CLAUDE.md files. variants/a.md and variants/b.md are the example variant files checked into the repo for local testing. You pass them as --a variants/a.md --b variants/b.md to clawmark run. Each variant is written as CLAUDE.md at the root of every cloned task workspace before Claude is invoked, so Claude reads that file as its system prompt/instructions for the task. The content difference between the two files is what drives the A/B comparison—clawmark measures whether those instructions lead to more or fewer resolved SWE-bench tasks.

tour.title: Build Configuration & Dependencies: Cargo.toml
tour.description: Cargo.toml declares the crate as a 2021-edition binary named clawmark, enforces unsafe_code = "forbid" via [lints.rust], and lists the minimal set of dependencies: clap (CLI parsing), serde + serde_json (serialization), sha2 + hex (variant fingerprinting), tempfile (sandbox temp directories), tokio (async runtime for parallel invocations), and tempfile. The project intentionally avoids anyhow, thiserror, tokio async I/O, and any model-output parsing. Cargo.lock pins exact versions for reproducible builds.

tour.title: CI Pipeline & End-to-End Smoke Test
tour.description: The GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/ci.yml runs cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, and cargo test on every push and pull request, keeping the codebase formatted and lint-clean without requiring Docker. The end-to-end integration test lives in scripts/e2e_smoke.sh—a Bash script that wires together all three subcommands (doctor, run, report) against a real Docker environment to verify the full pipeline works. The planning documents under .planning/ (PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md) capture the GSD workflow state and phase history for the maintainer.
